title: Mental Health Evaluation * Veterans - 211info
text: - Radius - 5 Miles 10 Miles 25 Miles 50 Miles 100 Miles Search by Provider Name > New Search > Mental & Behavioral Health > Mental Health Evaluation * Veterans 3 Matching Service Providers Refine Results - Select - Adolescents Children Adolescents Adoption/Foster Care/Kinship Care Issues AIDS/HIV Issues Children Domestic Violence Issues Grade School Students Hispanic/Latino Community Homelessness Issues Immigration Issues Infants/Toddlers LGBTQ2+ Issues Native American Community Older Adults Perinatal Depression Veterans Print & Share All Results X Print & Share Print PDF VA NORTHWEST HEALTH NETWORK PORTLAND VA MEDICAL CENTER HEALTH CARE SYSTEM VETERANS' JUSTICE OUTREACH PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF The purpose of the Veterans Justice Outreach (VJO) initiative is to avoid unnecessary criminalization of mental illness and extended incarceration among veterans by ensuring that eligible veterans in contact with the criminal justice system have access to Veterans Health Administration (VHA) mental health and substance abuse services. The VJO can assess needs and identify appropriate resources, refer and link qualified veterans to appropriate services, and liaise between VA and courts, attorneys, and community partners. The VJO cannot provide legal adcice, perfom forensic psychological evaluations for the cours, accept custody of a veteran, or guarantee VA program acceptance. 3710 SW US Veterans Hospital Road Portland, OR 97239 Eligibility: Justice-involved veterans Hours: Monday-Friday 7:30am-4pm (503) 721-1025 Get Directions Visit Website More Details VA NORTHWEST HEALTH NETWORK SOUTHERN OREGON VA REHABILITATION CENTER AND CLINICS VETERANS' JUSTICE OUTREACH PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF The purpose of the Veterans Justice Outreach (VJO) initiative is to avoid unnecessary criminalization of mental illness and extended incarceration among veterans by ensuring that eligible veterans in contact with the criminal justice system have access to Veterans Health Administration (VHA) mental health and substance abuse services. The VJO can assess needs and identify appropriate resources, refer and link qualified veterans to appropriate services, and liaise between VA and courts, attorneys, and community partners.
